title: C Sorting Algorithms
content:
### Sorting Algorithms in C

Sorting arranges elements in a specific order (ascending or descending).

### Bubble Sort

Simple but slow. Repeatedly swaps adjacent elements if in wrong order.

- Time: O(n^2)
- Space: O(1)

### Selection Sort

Finds minimum element and places it at the beginning.

- Time: O(n^2)
- Space: O(1)

### Insertion Sort

Builds sorted array one element at a time.

- Time: O(n^2), O(n) for nearly sorted
- Space: O(1)

### Quick Sort

Divide-and-conquer. Picks a pivot and partitions around it.

- Time: O(n log n) average, O(n^2) worst
- Space: O(log n)

### Merge Sort

Divide-and-conquer. Divides into halves, sorts, and merges.

- Time: O(n log n)
- Space: O(n)

---

### Algorithm Comparison

| Algorithm | Best Case | Average | Worst Case | Space | Stable |
|-----------|-----------|---------|------------|-------|--------|
| Bubble | O(n) | O(n^2) | O(n^2) | O(1) | Yes |
| Selection | O(n^2) | O(n^2) | O(n^2) | O(1) | No |
| Insertion | O(n) | O(n^2) | O(n^2) | O(1) | Yes |
| Quick | O(n log n) | O(n log n) | O(n^2) | O(log n) | No |
| Merge | O(n log n) | O(n log n) | O(n log n) | O(n) | Yes |

### Built-in qsort()

```c
#include <stdlib.h>
void qsort(void *base, size_t num, size_t size,
           int (*cmp)(const void *, const void *));
```

---

> **Key Point:** Use `qsort()` from `<stdlib.h>` for general sorting. For specific needs, choose the algorithm based on data size, stability requirements, and memory constraints.
